title: Rendering Optimization
description: Optimize rendering pipelines: draw calls, instancing, LODs, occlusion culling, and GPU profiling.
learningObjectives: Differentiate rendering bottlenecks: Draw Calls (CPU) vs Pixel Shading (GPU)
Implement Level of Detail (LOD) reductions for dense geometry meshes
Profile render states using GPU debugging tools like RenderDoc

sections.title: Draw Calls and Mesh Instancing
sections.content: A Draw Call is a command from the CPU to the GPU directing it to render a mesh. Every draw call introduces CPU overhead. Batching multiple meshes into a single draw call via Instanced Static Meshes (ISM) is critical for rendering dense fields (like grass or trees) without frame rate degradation. Level of Detail (LOD) systems swap high-poly meshes with lower-poly versions as distance from camera increases, saving vertex calculation costs.

sections.title: Instanced Static Meshes
sections.content: Instancing identical foliage meshes in Level blueprints. Instead of spawning 500 separate Static Mesh Actors (each triggering a unique draw call), you add a single `InstancedStaticMeshComponent` and register 500 transform offsets, rendering the entire batch in a single draw command.
